title: quercus pachyloma
intro: Quercus pachyloma, also known as the red stinkwood, is a species of tree in the beech family (Fagaceae) native to tropical Africa. The bark of Quercus pachyloma has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, primarily for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Quercus pachyloma have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections. Herbal preparations of Quercus pachyloma include infusions of the bark and leaves, which can be used to treat a range of ailments.
